description: NOTE - This special presentation is NOT included with either Full Fest or VIP Full Fest passes and tickets must be purchased separately. Join us on Friday, June 26 after Ship Point comes to a close and Victoria Ska & Reggae Fest transforms ENCORE for Dubwise & Dancehall, a Jamaican night out - where the dancefloor becomes culture in motion with bold style, fearless movement, and unity in every lyric.Curated by local reggae icon Caleb Hart, Dubwise it will be a form of cultural and sonic teleportation. From deep dubwise pressure, sub-bass rolling through the room, to explosive dancehall energy. Our world-class DJs dancehall general Nico Supreme, Canadas jungle king Marcus Visionary and DJ Real West will be taking you on a journey from classic riddims to modern anthems. Were also excited to welcome 2025 Juno Nominee SKYSTAR, who has been defining Canadian reggae on a global stage, blazing his trail from the icy streets of Alberta, with a fresh sound and unmatched energy. title: SOULS OF MISCHIEF, CHARLY BLACK, EVA LAZARUS, NICO SUPREME, SONIC ALLEY, TANK GYAL
description: Saturday in the Inner Harbour at Ship Point is always a special day during Ska Del the Funky Homosapien, Casual, Pep Love and the Souls of Mischief Opio, Tajai, Phesto and A-Plus. The quartets landmark album 93 Til Infinity remains one of the touchstones of the genre, with its live bass and deep jazz samples. Their intricate, introspective rhymes and flows changed the game in a way that people are only now beginning to recognize en masse. A west coast summer night at Ship Point with Souls of Mischief is something you do not want to miss.Where else can you see not just a legendary hip-hop, but see them with a living legend of dancehall on the bill as well? Because thats what CHARLY BLACK is, a living legend of dancehall. Theres no other way to define such a powerful voice. Songs like Whine and Youre Perfect are huge dancehall tracks and have helped define the genre, but its his intergalactically popular track Gyal You A Party Animal that vaulted Black into international stardom. description: aurorawave is a boundary-pushing rock band recognized for pioneering a distinct sound they term reggaecore a fusion of heavy guitar-driven intensity and rhythmic influences drawn from reggae. Their music integrates aggressive, groove-oriented arrangements with emotionally charged lyricism, resulting in a style that is both musically intricate and viscerally accessible. Formed with the intention of absolute authenticity, aurorawave established a clear identity with their self-titled debut album. The project featured notable collaborations with members of The Ghost Inside, Atreyu, and Attila, introducing the bands hybrid sound to a broad spectrum of alternative and heavy music audiences. Their debut positioned them as emerging voices with the versatility to operate across multiple subcultures within the rock and reggae landscapes.In 2025, the band unveiled Monument, a decisive step forward in their evolution. H.R. of Bad Brains & The Human Rights Movement
description: Paul "HR" Hudson is best known as the frontman for the iconic punk band Bad Brains. HR and his brother, Earl, along with friends Gary "Dr. Know" Miller and Darryl Jenifer, began as Mind Power, a jazz fusion band, and later formed Bad Brains in 1979. With unprecedented speed and precision, Bad Brains took the Washington, DC music scene by storm and quickly became the most influential band in American punk history. They created music that was about closing the racial divide and maintaining a positive mental attitude (PMA). This concept of PMA continues to inspire fans to this day.

title: The Wailers - 50 Years of Positive Vibrations
description: The Wailers return to Canada to celebrate 50 years of Rastaman Vibration, marking a defining moment in reggae history and honouring one of Bob Marley most influential albums. The 50 Years of Positive Vibrations tour highlights select cuts from Rastaman Vibration alongside timeless classics that continue to unite audiences across generations.Audiences can expect a soul-stirring setlist weaving together the revolutionary spirit of Rastaman Vibration, classic anthems, and select modern highlights delivered with the same authenticity, musicianship, and heart that made The Wailers a cornerstone of global music.With Special Guest OK VYBEDoors: 6:30pmShow 9:00pm Additional information www.meloproductions.ca

title: The Wailers 50 Years of Positive Vibrations
description: The Wailers, Bob Marleys former Band, return to Canada to celebrate 50 years of Rastaman Vibration, marking a defining moment in reggae history and honoring one of Bob Marley most influential albums. The 50 Years of Positive Vibrations tour highlights select cuts from Rastaman Vibration alongside timeless classics that continue to unite audiences across generations.Released in 1976, Rastaman Vibration carried reggaes message of resistance, spirituality, and hope onto the global stage, with songs like Positive Vibration, Roots, Rock, Reggae, and War remaining as relevant today as ever. Under the leadership of Aston Barrett Jr., son of legendary bassist Aston Familyman Barrett, The Wailers continue to honor their roots while carrying the music forward with purpose and authenticity.Audiences can expect a soul-stirring setlist weaving together the revolutionary spirit of Rastaman Vibration, classic anthems, and select modern highlights delivered with the same authenticity, musicianship, an

title: Fortunate Youth - Day 1
description: From backyard BBQs in Southern California beach towns to its rise as one of the most popular reggae rock bands in the country, Fortunate Youth remains devoted to its roots and a simple mission: Spread peace, love, and unity with music. Its a mantra of sorts for the multi-faceted sextet from Hermosa Beach, driving a relentless thirst for live performances and an ever-evolving cache of original material dotted with influences of blues, ska, rock, world, soul, and punk.Dedicated to performing live, the ensemble has logged countless miles across the globe, with F.Y. supporting brethren such as Slightly Stoopid, Stick Figure, and SOJA.

title: Landon McNamara
description: Landon McNamara can feel it. Big things are coming. The singer-songwriter and guitarist from Hawaiis North Shore is on the cusp of a major swell- familiar waters for this accomplished surfer- but this wave is one of song and soul.McNamaras newest drop, the laid back yet deeply spiritual If You Only Knew, renews the artist with strains of acoustic reggae and Hawaiian soul, underpinning a thoughtful recitation on the value of life, and ultimately, the power of love. The only promised thing to us all is death. And at the end of our life, when youre taking that last breath, would you be satisfied with how you spent your time? asks McNamara. I wanna motivate people to realize life isnt about money, or shiny things, or social status. Its about experiences; creating beautiful memories; family; friends; finding out who you truly are and living that existence to the fullest. Above all, its about love.
